heading: Repealed. ,July 3, 1950, ch. 446, § 5(2)64 Stat. 320
label: 824 to 844 Repealed. ,July 3, 1950, ch. 446, § 5(2)64 Stat. 320
notes: Section 824, , , related to passage of bills and their alterations or amendments.act Mar. 2, 1917, ch. 145, § 3439 Stat. 961
Section 825, , , related to reference of bills to committees, signature by governor, and approval by President.act Mar. 2, 1917, ch. 145, § 3439 Stat. 960

Section 826, acts , ; , ; , §§ 1, 2, ; , , required laws enacted by Legislature of Puerto Rico to be reported to Congress.Mar. 2, 1917, ch. 145, § 3439 Stat. 961May 29, 1928, ch. 901, § 1(128)45 Stat. 996Feb. 28, 1929, ch. 36445 Stat. 1348May 17, 1932, ch. 19047 Stat. 158
Section 827, , , related to time of taking effect of laws and to introduction of a bill.act Mar. 2, 1917, ch. 145, § 3439 Stat. 961
Section 828, , , related to house journals.act Mar. 2, 1917, ch. 145, § 3439 Stat. 961
Section 829, , , required sessions of each house and committees to be open.act Mar. 2, 1917, ch. 145, § 3439 Stat. 961
Section 830, , , related to adjournment.act Mar. 2, 1917, ch. 145, § 3439 Stat. 961
Section 831, , , required presiding officer to sign bills and resolutions.act Mar. 2, 1917, ch. 145, § 3439 Stat. 962
Section 832, , , provided that bills, with the exception of appropriation bills, were to contain one subject.act Mar. 2, 1917, ch. 145, § 3439 Stat. 962
Section 833, , , provided that revenue bills were to originate in house of representatives.act Mar. 2, 1917, ch. 145, § 3439 Stat. 962
Section 834, , , related to appropriation bills for executive, legislative and judicial departments.act Mar. 2, 1917, ch. 145, § 3439 Stat. 962
Section 835, , , related to revival or amendment of laws.act Mar. 2, 1917, ch. 145, § 3439 Stat. 962
Section 836, , , required legislature to prescribe number, duties and compensation of officers and employees.act Mar. 2, 1917, ch. 145, § 3439 Stat. 962
Section 837, , , prohibited bills giving extra compensation for services rendered.act Mar. 2, 1917, ch. 145, § 3439 Stat. 962

Section 838, acts , ; , prohibited laws relating to extension of term of office of officials, double jobs and salary of senators or representatives during term of office.Mar. 2, 1917, ch. 145, § 3439 Stat. 962June 24, 1948, ch. 610, § 362 Stat. 580
Section 839, , , related to presentation of orders, resolutions, etc., to Governor.act Mar. 2, 1917, ch. 145, § 3439 Stat. 962
Section 840, , , required Governor to submit a financial budget at opening of each regular session of the legislature.act Mar. 2, 1917, ch. 145, § 3439 Stat. 960

Section 841, acts , ; , , provided for order of payment of appropriations, where revenue insufficient to meet appropriations, and limited appropriations under certain conditions.Mar. 2, 1917, ch. 145, § 3439 Stat. 962May 17, 1932, ch. 19047 Stat. 158
Section 842, acts , ; , , required Governor to transmit copies of laws to executive department of United States.Mar. 2, 1917, ch. 145, § 2339 Stat. 958May 17, 1932, ch. 19047 Stat. 158
Section 843, acts , ; , , related to definition and punishment of the offense of corrupt solicitation.Mar. 2, 1917, ch. 145, § 3439 Stat. 962May 17, 1932, ch. 19047 Stat. 158
Section 844, , , related to punishment for offense of influencing officials by bribery.act Mar. 2, 1917, ch. 145, § 3439 Stat. 962
